detailed_description: This 3D animation provides a detailed cross-section view of human skin, showcasing the intricate layers, blood vessels, and hair follicles. The animation zeroes in on a single hair follicle, illustrating the biological mechanism where Dihydrotestosterone (DHT) molecules accumulate around its base. The visual representation, complete with a 'DHT' label, effectively communicates how this hormone interacts with and potentially impacts hair follicles, offering insight into a common cause of hair loss.

segments.why_kept: This entire segment is the semantic core, demonstrating the detailed anatomical structure of the skin and, most importantly, the animated mechanism of Dihydrotestosterone (DHT) affecting a hair follicle, which is the central informational point of the video. The animation clearly illustrates the process and labels the key hormone, making it a crucial 'Mechanism_Of_Action' moment without any extraneous footage.
